skill.name: teach
skill.description: Create a stateful teaching workspace with missions, lessons, resources, glossary entries, and learning records.
skill.summary: Turns a workspace into a structured, citation-driven learning environment.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the shell execution, weak crypto, and reconnaissance matches are false positives from Markdown templates, filenames, and prose. The remaining risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to create persistent workspace files, produce HTML lessons, use citations, and suggest external communities.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Workspace File Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create and update mission, resource, reference, lesson, notes, and learning-record files. This is expected behavior, but users should review generated files before relying on them or publishing generated HTML.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing behavior is explicit across SKILL.md and the format files. The intent appears educational rather than malicious, so the risk is operational rather than a confirmed attack.

medium_findings.title: External Research and Community Recommendations
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to gather trusted resources, cite external links, and find communities for real-world learning. This can trigger browsing or external link suggestions, but no credential collection or data exfiltration was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly encourages use of external resources and communities. I found no evidence that it sends local data to a remote endpoint or requires unauthorized network access.

medium_findings.title: Optional Local Command to Open Lessons
medium_findings.description: The skill says opening a generated lesson should be easy, ideally with one CLI command. This could lead to local command suggestions, but the skill does not provide executable scripts or dynamic command construction.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of a CLI command recommendation. The actual command is not specified, so command execution risk depends on future agent behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown file names, directory names, and fenced examples rather than Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain Markdown literals and templates, not code that executes commands. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The flagged lines contain filenames, descriptions, or educational prose, with no cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, cipher configuration, or hashing code. The static analyzer appears to have matched harmless text patterns.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are sample resources in a RESOURCES.md template. They are not contacted by code and do not receive local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside a Markdown example block for user-curated resources. There is no fetch call, webhook, or network client code.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are false positives. The flagged lines discuss user goals and example exercise topics, not probing hosts or collecting environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain teaching guidance only. I found no commands or instructions to scan networks, inspect systems, or enumerate local infrastructure.

content.value_statement: Many learning sessions lose context between lessons and fail to connect practice to a clear goal. This skill creates a workspace with missions, lessons, resources, and records that guide future teaching.
actual_capabilities: Creates a mission document that explains why the user wants to learn a topic.
Structures lessons as focused HTML files saved in a lessons directory.
Tracks learning records for demonstrated understanding, prior knowledge, and corrected misconceptions.
Maintains a curated resources document with trusted knowledge sources and communities.
Builds glossary and reference documents for reusable concepts and quick review.
Uses past records to choose lessons near the user's current ability level.
limitations: It depends on the agent to create files correctly in the current workspace.
It does not include source files, scripts, or a dedicated learning application.
External resources still need human judgment for accuracy and suitability.
It is not a substitute for professional instruction in regulated or high-risk topics.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Learning Goal
use_cases.description: Define a concrete mission, success criteria, constraints, and first lesson for a topic the learner wants to study over time.

use_cases.title: Teach Technical Concepts Incrementally
use_cases.target_user: Developer using Claude Code or Codex
use_cases.description: Create short HTML lessons, reference notes, and learning records while learning a programming language, framework, or tool.

use_cases.title: Maintain Coaching Context
use_cases.description: Record what a learner already understands, what changed, and which resources should guide the next session.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Learning Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me start learning {topic}. Interview me about my goal, then create a mission and suggest the first small lesson.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Focused Lesson
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the next lesson for my mission. Teach one practical concept, include a short exercise, and record any learning evidence afterward.

prompt_templates.title: Update My Learning Records
prompt_templates.prompt: Review what I just demonstrated about {topic}. Add a learning record only if it changes what future lessons should assume.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Resource-Grounded Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my mission, resources, glossary, and learning records. Identify gaps, add trusted resources, and propose the next three lessons.

output_examples.input: I want to learn Rust so I can ship a small command-line tool for my team.
output: A mission document with the real work goal, constraints, and success criteria.
A first lesson about project setup and a tiny executable command.
A resources list with trusted Rust references and when to use each one.

output_examples.input: I already understand basic strength training, but I keep stalling on programming progressions.
output: A learning record that captures prior knowledge and the plateau problem.
A focused lesson on progressive overload choices for the learner's current training context.
Glossary entries only after the learner can use the terms correctly.

output_examples.input: My goal changed. I no longer need theory first. I need a practical interview preparation plan.
output: An updated mission that reflects the new outcome.
A learning record explaining why the change matters.
A revised sequence of short lessons tied to interview practice.

best_practices: Start by clarifying the mission before writing lessons or resources.
Keep each lesson focused on one concrete skill or concept.
Record learning only when the user shows evidence of understanding.
anti_patterns: Creating broad lessons that cover many unrelated concepts at once.
Adding resources without notes about trust, scope, and when to use them.
Treating learning records as session logs instead of decision-grade insights.

faq.question: Does this skill create files?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to create and update mission, lesson, resource, glossary, notes, and learning-record files in the workspace.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it teach any topic?
faq.answer: It provides a teaching workflow for many topics, but the quality depends on the resources and agent judgment.

faq.question: How does it choose the next lesson?
faq.answer: It uses the mission, prior learning records, and the user's current ability to select a focused next step.

faq.question: Does it browse the web automatically?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to gather trusted resources when needed, but browsing depends on the host tool and user permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice. The main risks are workspace file creation, generated HTML, and external resource recommendations.
